_comment: Issue #64 plan v2 Δ2: a vendored file using any deferred directive is skipped loudly and wholesale, with a concrete activation issue per blocking directive. Issue #154 made the block-expect forms (expect ordered, expect range vector) and load_with_nhcb EXECUTABLE — the only deferred directives left are eval_warn/eval_info (no pinned file uses them). The corpus test fails if a listed directive disappears from its file (activate the file!), if an unlisted deferred directive appears, or if a file is absent from both the executed set and this manifest. `manual_skip` (issue #124, M7-A6) is the non-directive lever: a file with ZERO deferred directives that still fails on replay for reasons outside any tracked directive — no drift check applies to it, unlike blocking_directives. Current users: none — all 21 pinned upstream files are active. The final three were de-listed by #164 (classic-bucket histogram_quantile lowest-bucket interpolation ported) and by #163/#165, whose two \xHH byte-escape rows are recorded in eval-divergences.jsonl per the #165 owner ruling.
